page: OVHcloud b3-640 is a General Purpose server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by x86_64 architecture AMD EPYC Milan processors. It features 160 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 640.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance is equipped with a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates exceptional multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU scores all ranking in the top tier. The memory subsystem also demonstrates top-tier performance in PassMark memory tests and small-block read-only memory bandwidth. Single-core performance remains strong, placing in the top 25% across stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. The server excels at parallel processing tasks, such as software compilation with Clang and ray tracing, both of which achieve top-tier benchmark results.

While the server delivers strong performance in database operations and static web serving, as well as top-tier throughput in Redis SET operations, certain workloads reveal performance trade-offs. Large language model prompt processing is strong to top-tier across small, medium, and large models, but text generation speeds drop to average for small and medium models, and fall into the bottom 10% for large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Consequently, this instance is highly suited for high-density multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database hosting, and web serving, but is not recommended for large-scale LLM text generation.
description: OVHcloud b3-640 is a General Purpose server featuring 160 dedicated physical AMD EPYC Milan cores on an x86_64 architecture, operating at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 640.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and Redis SET operations. While single-core performance and memory bandwidth are strong, the lack of GPU acceleration results in poor text generation speeds for large 70B LLM models, though prompt processing remains efficient. This server is highly suited for highly parallel workloads, software development pipelines, database management, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-640 is a General Purpose server with 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 640 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-640 features 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 640 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for high-density multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated 160-core AMD EPYC server offering balanced memory and NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with 1.0 thread per core.
640 GB of system memory paired with a 400 GB local NVMe SSD.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address.
Excellent fit for databases, Redis caching, and parallel CI/CD pipelines.
Underperforms in large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU.
